question: Will there be a space station with artificial gravity by June 2026?
reason: No space station with artificial gravity exists or is scheduled to exist by June 2026.
evidence: The International Space Station (ISS) continues to operate without artificial gravity, with no plans for such a feature. ([nasa.gov](https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/spacestation/2026/02/05/dragon-preps-artificial-intelligence-and-medical-gear-fill-crews-day/?utm_source=openai))
Russia patented a design for a space station with artificial gravity, but it is not expected to be operational by June 2026. ([daily-sun.com](https://www.daily-sun.com/20/848128?utm_source=openai))

The Voyager Space Station, a proposed commercial space station with artificial gravity, is scheduled to begin construction in 2026, but it is not expected to be operational by June 2026. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_space_stations?utm_source=openai))
